content.method: Not resolvable by empirical method alone; requires explicit deliberative or value-elicitation processes (e.g., structured public deliberation, legislative debate, multi-criteria decision analysis making trade-off weights explicit) rather than additional causal estimation.
content.question: Should tracking be judged primarily by whether it reduces mean population performance while raising equity, or by whether it merely redistributes a fixed level of achievement across the distribution — and how should policymakers weigh mean-performance risk against equity gains when the two goals conflict?
content.why_it_matters: This question exposes a normative trade-off (how much aggregate performance loss, if any, is acceptable to achieve equity gains) rather than a purely empirical uncertainty; different value weightings on efficiency versus equity will lead to different policy choices even with identical evidence on effect sizes.
